title: campanula sibirica subsp. caucasica
intro: Campanula sibirica subsp. caucasica, also known as Siberian bellflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties. The roots and rhizomes of Campanula sibirica subsp. caucasica are used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to reap the plant's health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting respiratory health.
